こんな所で会えるなんて……こうして、また君と言葉を交わせるなんて……。: To meet her in a place like this... to be able to exchange words with her again like this...
運命的……そんな大仰な言葉さえ素直に受け入れられた。: Fateful... I could honestly accept even such a grandiose word.
宏治「どうしたんですか？ こんな場所で」: Koji: “What are you doing? In a place like this.”
だが、そんな思いとは裏腹に、俺の口から出たのはそんなツマラナイ一言だった。: But despite those feelings, what came out of my mouth was just such a boring sentence.
かれん「べ、別にサボってたわけじゃないから……」: Karen: “I-It's not like I was slacking off or anything...”
宏治「そんな風に思ってませんよ」: Koji: “ I don't think that at all.”
（う……やっぱり出だしがまずかった……何だこの会話は）: (Ugh... the start was bad after all... what kind of conversation is this.)
俺が密かに懊悩していると、かれんがふと海の方を見て口を開いた。: As I was secretly agonizing, Karen suddenly looked toward the sea and spoke.
かれん「疲れた時とか、いやになった時とか、たまにここに来て海を見ながら深呼吸をす: Karen: “When I'm tired, or when I've had enough, I occasionally come here to watch the sea and take a deep breath...”
その優しい声に包まれていると、さっきまでの複雑な思いが少しずつ溶けていくような気: Being enveloped in that kind voice, I felt as if the complicated feelings I had until now were gradually melting away.
かれん「……そうすると、何だか全部帳消しになっちゃうんだ……私って単純だから……: Karen: “...When I do that, everything somehow gets cancelled out... because I'm simple...”
かれんに問いただしたいことはたくさんあった。でも、今はそんな気分にはならなかった: There were many things I wanted to question Karen about. But I wasn't in the mood for that now.
そんな事で、この時間を台無しにしたくはない。: I didn't want to ruin this time with such things.
それに、ここでそれを聞いてしまうと、何か大きなものが崩れ去ってしまう気がして、そ: Besides, I was afraid that if I asked her here, something big would crumble away...
かれんが海の方に顔を向けたまま、問いかけてきた。: Karen asked while still facing toward the sea.
かれん「今度、ご飯食べにいこ？」: Karen: “Want to go out for a meal next time?”
あの日と同じセリフをもう一度つぶやく。: She muttered the same line from that day once again.
俺はかれんの顔も見ずに、静かに肯いた。: Without even looking at Karen's face, I nodded quietly.
（このまま、時が止まればいいのに……）: (If only time would stop like this...)
心からそう思った。: I thought that from the bottom of my heart.
彼女も、俺と同じように考えていることを願いながら……。: Wishing that she was thinking the same thing as me...
かれんと公園で会った翌日……。: The day after meeting Karen at the park...
外出前のオフィスで、他の営業マンが取引先に出払ってしまった後でも、俺は何となく躊: Even after the other salesmen had left for their clients and I was in the office before heading out, I was hesitating for some reason.
昨日、かれんと話しているうちに俺の中にあったわだかまりのようなものが和らいでいた: It's true that while talking to Karen yesterday, the resentment that was inside me had softened.
だが、あのとき彼女のこぼした言葉が一本の棘のように残って、俺の心はかき乱れていた: But the words she let out then remained like a thorn, and my heart was in turmoil.
かれん『疲れた時とか、いやになった時とか、たまにここに来て海を見ながら深呼吸をす: Karen: “When I'm tired, or when I've had enough, I occasionally come here to watch the sea and take a deep breath...”
かれん『……そうすると、何だか全部帳消しになっちゃうんだ……私って単純だから……: Karen: “...When I do that, everything somehow gets cancelled out... because I'm simple...”
……かれんがあそこに居た、それは彼女に取って『帳消しにしたい何かがあった』という: ...The fact that Karen was there, didn't it mean that for her, there was ‘something she wanted to cancel out’...
そしてそれは、やはり沢田との……。: And that was, as I thought, with Sawada...
そんな事を考えながら、意味もなくパソコン画面を眺めていた時だった。: It was when I was staring at the computer screen meaninglessly while thinking about such things.
佐藤部長「おい、宮坂。ちょっと……今、いいか？」: Director Sato: “Hey, Miyasaka. Do you... have a moment?”
佐藤部長が会議室の方を指差す。: Manager Sato pointed toward the meeting room.
突然の呼び出しに俺は少し驚いた。でも、この有り様だ……当然といえば当然だ。: I was a bit surprised by the sudden summons. But given my state, it was only natural.
正直言って婦長と沢田の情事を目撃して以来、仕事に身が入らずに、何をやってもうまく: To be honest, ever since I witnessed the tryst between the head nurse and Sawada, I haven't been able to put my heart into my work, and nothing I do goes well. My sales performance is probably plummeting too.
でも、どうにかしようという意欲すら湧かないでいた。: But I didn't even have the motivation to do anything about it.
（何だろう……またクレームか？）: (I wonder what it is... another complaint?)
タバコの匂いの充満する会議室……会議以外でここに来るなんていつぶりだろうか。: The meeting room filled with the smell of cigarettes... when was the last time I came here other than for a meeting?
後から入ってきた佐藤部長がドアを閉める。: Manager Sato, who entered after me, closed the door.
佐藤部長「いきなりすまんな……ああ、そんなに時間は取らせんから」: Director Sato: “Sorry for the suddenness... Ah, I won't take much of your time.”
そう聞いたところで、ホッとできるものでもない。長々と説教される方がまだマシかも知: Even hearing that didn't make me feel relieved. A long lecture might have been better.
まさかクビってこともないだろうが……。: I don't think I'll be fired, but...
佐藤部長「……お前、ここに来て何年になる？」: Director Sato: “...How many years has it been since you came here?”
宏治「はあ……今年で、三年になります」: Koji: “Um... it'll be three years this year.”
三年……一言で片付けるには重く退屈な日々だった……最近になって、ようやく日が差し: Three years... it's been a long and boring time to sum up in one word... it was just when I thought the sun was finally starting to shine through...
かれんの笑顔が、一瞬頭の中をよぎる……。: Karen's smiling face flashed through my mind for a moment...
佐藤部長「そうか、もう三年になるか。人間っていうものは同じ環境に慣れきってしまう: Director Sato: “I see, three years already. When people get too used to the same environment, nothing good comes of it...”
俺は佐藤部長のよく動く口元を、まるで無声映画でも見ているような感覚でただ眺めてい: I just watched Manager Sato's busy mouth, as if I were watching a silent movie.
その口から発せられる言葉の多くが、頭の中を素通りしていく。: Many of the words uttered from that mouth passed right through my head.
佐藤部長「……と、いうことで、担当先を替わってもらう……」: Director Sato: “...So, I'm having you change your accounts...”
まるで何気ない雑談の延長のような流れの中で、俺にとっては致命的とも言える言葉がぶ: In a flow that was like an extension of casual small talk, words that were fatal for me were thrown my way.
高橋先生からの苦情か、あるいは……どっちにしろ俺の勤務態度に問題があるからだろう: Was it a complaint from Dr. Takahashi, or... either way, it's probably because there's a problem with my work attitude...
理由は言えないが、食い下がる: I can't say why, but I'll stand my ground
『冗談じゃないです、そんなの到底受け入れられません！』: “You've got to be joking, I can't possibly accept that!”
と強く言い返せたら、どんなにかいいだろう。: How good it would be if I could say that strongly.
でも、この俺に選択の余地など……無い。: But for me, there was... no choice.
そう答えるしかないじゃないか……。: I had no choice but to answer that...
佐藤部長「後任は小林だ。特に引き継ぎもいらんだろう……ついでに俺も、久しぶりに向: Director Sato: “Kobayashi will be your successor. You won't need to do any handovers in particular... I'm thinking of going there to give my greetings after a long time, too. So, you should finish up your brief greetings today.”
部長の言葉を聴きながら、大きな喪失感と安堵……相反する２つの思いが一気に訪れた。: Listening to the Manager's words, a great sense of loss and relief... two conflicting thoughts arrived at once.
（これで良かったのかもしれない……それは分かっている）: (Maybe this was for the best... I know that.)
このまま、かれんと会えなくなる……その事実が受け入れられなかった。: I won't be able to see Karen like this... I couldn't accept that fact.
……彼女の存在が俺の中で消滅してしまうこと……それは、とても恐ろしいことのような: ...The fact that her existence would vanish from within me... I felt as if that were something very terrifying.
佐藤部長「おい……どうした？ 宮坂。わかったのか？」: Director Sato: “Hey... what's wrong? Miyasaka. Did you understand?”
かれんの笑顔、かれんの喋り方、そして……沢田とからみ合うかれんの姿……そこで、俺: Karen's smile, Karen's way of talking, and... Karen entangled with Sawada... that's where my thoughts were cut off...
そう言って頭を下げると、俺は力なく会議室を後にした。: Bowing as I said that, I left the meeting room listlessly.
挨拶か……気が重くなる。一体、どんな顔をしてかれんに会えばいいんだ……。: Greetings, huh... my heart feels heavy. Just what kind of face should I show when I meet Karen...
潮風に髪をなびかせるかれんの姿が頭に浮かび、俺は深い溜息をついて目を閉じた。: The image of Karen with her hair fluttering in the sea breeze floated in my head, and I let out a deep sigh and closed my eyes.
見慣れたはずの景色……でも、今日は建物自体が俺を拒絶しているように見えてしまう…: A sight that should be familiar... but today the building itself looks as if it's rejecting me...
（まぁ、そんな事を考えていても仕方が無い……まずは、高橋先生に挨拶をするのが筋だ: (Well, it can't be helped even if I think about that... first, it's only right to greet Dr. Takahashi...)
旧館のナースステーションの前を通りかかった時に、長妻婦長の姿が見えた。: When I passed by the nurse station in the old wing, I saw Head Nurse Nagatsuma.
（声をかけづらいのは確かだが、何も言わないのもまずいか……）: (It's true that it's hard to call out to her, but it's bad not to say anything, too...)
宏治「長妻婦長、この度担当を替わることになりまして……」: Koji: “Head Nurse Nagatsuma, I'm going to be changing accounts...”
麗子「ああ、ミヤマさん……お話は聞いてるわ。今までご苦労様。ちょっと急ぐので、こ: Reiko: “Ah, Miyama-san... I've heard. Thanks for your hard work until now. I'm in a bit of a hurry, so please excuse me.”
俺の挨拶を最後まで聞かず、一気にそう言って、婦長は背を向けて歩き去ってしまった。: Without listening to the end of my greeting, she said that all at once and turned her back to walk away.
いつにも増して冷たい声……それに、俺と目を合わせようともしなかった。: Her voice was colder than ever... and she didn't even try to meet my eyes.
（やれやれ、最後までこんな調子かよ……）: (Good grief, even at the end, she's like this...)
背の高いナース「あ、ミヤマさん。担当、替わるんだって？ なんか寂しくなるねぇ……: Tall Nurse: “Ah, Miyama-san. You're changing accounts? It's going to be lonely... Ah, speaking of which, Emiko came to give her greetings earlier, too.”
心の中で毒づきながら婦長の背中を見送っていると、顔なじみのナースが声を掛けてきた: While I was cursing the head nurse's back in my heart, a nurse I knew called out to me.
結局、あの日に何が起こったのかはっきりわからないままに、彼女も俺もこの病院を離れ: In the end, without clearly knowing what happened on that day, both she and I will be leaving this hospital. And Karen and Sawada will remain...
童顔のナース「宮坂さん、お元気で。病気になったら、この病院に来てくださいね」: Baby-Faced Nurse: “Miyasaka-san, take care. If you get sick, please come to this hospital.”
ぽっちゃりしたナース「馬鹿ね、『元気で』って言ったそばから何言ってんのよ。宮坂さ: Chubby Nurse: “You idiot, what are you saying right after telling him to ‘take care’. Miyasaka-san, thanks for your hard work. Please take care.”
童顔のナース「あ、それもそっか、あははははは」: Baby-Faced Nurse: “Ah, that's true, too, hahaha.”
続けて何人かのナースが顔を出し、挨拶に来たのか雑談しに来たのか分からない状態にな: Several nurses appeared one after another, and it became unclear whether they had come to give their greetings or to chat.
（まったく……この人たちも相変わらずだよな。取りあえず話題を変えないと、いつまで: (Honestly... these people are the same as ever. If I don't change the subject, it'll probably go on forever...)
宏治「そう言えば……今日、高橋先生はどちらにいらっしゃいますか？」: Koji: “By the way... where is Dr. Takahashi today?”
彼女たちの会話が途切れるのを見計らって、そう聞いてみる。: I asked when their conversation paused.
背の高いナース「高橋先生？ 今日は学会で丸一日居ないはずだけど」: Tall Nurse: “Dr. Takahashi? He shouldn't be here all day today due to an academic conference.”
ぽっちゃりしたナース「あ、伝言を言い付かってます。『今までお疲れさん。あと、改め: Chubby Nurse: “Ah, I have a message for you. ‘Thanks for your work so far. Also, there's no need to come and give your greetings again.’”
宏治「そう……ですか。ありがとうございました、失礼します」: Koji: “I see... thank you very much, excuse me.”
俺は深々と一礼して、ナースステーションを後にした。: I bowed deeply and left the nurse station.
肝心な高橋先生は不在だったが、何となくほっとした。: The essential Dr. Takahashi was absent, but I felt relieved somehow.
『来る必要はないぞ』か……言外に『来るな』と言うことかもな。: ‘There's no need to come’... he might be implicitly saying ‘don't come.’
（まあ、最近は本当に滅茶苦茶だったものな……）: (Well, lately things have really been a mess...)
とにかく他の場所への挨拶を済ませてしまおう。: Anyway, let's finish the greetings at the other locations.
関係各所への挨拶は、思ったよりもアッサリ終わった。半年間担当していたといっても、: Greetings to the various departments ended more simply than I thought. Even though I had been in charge for six months, that's just how it is...
外科病棟へ挨拶に行くかどうか迷ったけれども、結局やめにした。: I hesitated about whether to go and give my greetings at the Surgery ward, but in the end, I decided against it.
（実際に取引のない外科に挨拶にいくのも、変な話だしな……）: (It's a strange story to go and give greetings to Surgery where I don't actually have any business...)
そんな風に自分に言い訳をする。: I made such an excuse to myself.
でも、本当の所を言うと、かれんに会うのが怖かった……。: But to tell the truth, I was afraid of meeting Karen...
玄関を出て駐車場へ向かう途中で立ち止まり、病院の方を振りかえる。: On the way out of the entrance toward the parking lot, I stopped and looked back toward the hospital.
本館と旧館をつなぐ渡り廊下の辺りに目をやると、その窓辺にこちらをじっと見つめてい: When I looked toward the connecting corridor between the main wing and the old wing, I felt as if I saw a figure staring intently this way by the window.
もちろん、何の根拠もない……だが、反射的にそう思った。: Of course, there was no grounding for it... but I thought so reflexively.
それにくるりと背を向けて、心の中で『さよなら』と呟く……それが、今の俺に出来る精: I turned my back and muttered “goodbye” in my heart... that was the best I could do right now.
（これでいいんだ、これで、よかったんだ……）: (This is fine, this was for the best...)
何度も自分に言い聞かせながら、今度は振り返ることなく病院を後にした。: While telling myself that over and over, this time I left the hospital without looking back.
あれから数週間──俺は新しい担当先の病院に足繁く通っていた。: Weeks had passed since then—I was frequently visiting the hospital of my new assignment.
おばさんナース「ああ、ミヤマさん、今日も元気ですねぇ」: Old Nurse: “Ah, Miyama-san, you’re looking energetic today as well.”
こっちの病院のナースは、本当に年配の女性ばかりだ。: The nurses at this hospital are truly nothing but older women.
無論そんな事はどうでもよかったし、むしろその方がよかった。仕事をしている間は余計: Of course, that didn't matter to me; in fact, it was better that way. While I'm working, I don't have to think about unnecessary things.
（こうやってどんどん忘れていくんだろうな……）: (I suppose I'll just keep forgetting her more and more like this...)
そんな寂しさも心の内に押し込め、何もかも忘れて仕事に熱中した。: I pushed even that loneliness deep into my heart and immersed myself in work, forgetting everything.
そのおかげか、最近はおかしいくらい簡単に営業成績があがっていっている。: Perhaps because of that, my sales performance has been rising recently with ridiculous ease.
そんなものだ。気持ちの入れようひとつで全てが変わる……。: That’s how it goes. With a single shift in mindset, everything changes...
久しぶりに仕事を早く終えることができた俺は、珍しく残業もせずに席を立つ。: Being able to finish work early for the first time in a while, I stood up from my seat without doing the usual overtime.
特に予定があるわけではない。ただ、このところ何も考えずに仕事ばかりしていたせいも: I didn't have any particular plans. It was just that lately, partly because I'd been doing nothing but working without thinking, I was feeling somewhat fatigued.
佐藤部長「お、宮坂！ 一杯どうだ？」: Director Sato: “Oh, Miyasaka! How about a drink?”
宏治「あ、すみません。ちょっと用事がありますんで……ではお先に失礼します！」: Koji: “Ah, I’m sorry. I have some business to attend to... Well then, excuse me!”
部長が上機嫌な様子で誘ってきたが、頭を下げてそれを断る。: The manager invited me in high spirits, but I bowed and declined.
担当が替わってから、俺の営業成績がうなぎ登りなのにご満悦なのだろうが、酒の肴にそ: He's probably satisfied since my sales performance has been skyrocketing after the change in assignment, but I can't stand the thought of having that conversation over drinks.
そんなことをされたら、思い出したくないことまで……思い出してしまいそうだった……: If that happened, I felt like I might... remember the things I don't want to remember...
会社を出た俺は、何となく繁華街へやって来た。: After leaving the office, I wandered aimlessly into the downtown district.
色とりどりのネオンサインが輝き、着飾った女の子たちの笑い声と、客待ちのタクシーの: Colorful neon signs glowed, the laughter of dressed-up girls and the honking of taxis waiting for customers filled the air everywhere, showing the glamorous face unique to a weekend night.
でも、そんな中に身を置いてさえ『遊びに行きたい』なんて気持ちはカケラも起こらなか: But even being in the middle of it, I didn't feel even a fragment of a desire to ‘go out and play.’
（この歳にして、もうすでにご隠居気分だな……）: (At this age, I already feel like a retired old man...)
そんな事を考え、一人苦笑いをする。: Thinking that, I gave a lonely, bitter smile.
（さて、家にでも帰って溜まっている家事全般でも片付けることにしようか……）: (Well then, I guess I'll head home and take care of the general housework that's been piling up...)
元々俺は几帳面な方だったのだが、最近は忙しかったのと精神的なショックもあったせい: Originally, I was quite the meticulous type, but lately, perhaps due to being busy and the mental shock, I’d become rather slovenly.
（いつまでも、引きずっていちゃいけない……新しい一歩を踏み出さなくっちゃな……）: (I can't keep dragging this out forever... I have to take a new step forward...)
駅前のスーパーに寄って『半額』のシールの貼ってある惣菜と缶ビールを買い、久しぶり: I stopped by the supermarket in front of the station, bought some side dishes with ‘Half Price’ stickers and a can of beer, and walked home with a light step, enjoying my early return for once.
アパートの入り口に誰かが立っている。: Someone is standing at the entrance of the apartment.
暗がりの中で顔までは確認できなかったけれども、シルエットからすると女だ……。: I couldn't confirm the face in the darkness, but from the silhouette, it's a woman...
かれん『宮坂さん、お帰り！ 約束通り一緒にご飯食べに来たよ』: Karen: “Miyasaka-san, welcome back! I came to have dinner together, just like I promised.”
有り得ないものを見て、思わず立ち止まってしまう。: Seeing something that shouldn't be possible, I reflexively stopped in my tracks.
何だ、今のは？ 俺は一体何を見たんだ？: What was that just now? What on earth did I see?
（馬鹿馬鹿しい！ ただアパートの前に女が立っているだけじゃないか。何だってあんな: (How ridiculous! It’s just a woman standing in front of the apartment. Why did I see something like that...)
でも、今さっきの幻で気づいたことがあった。: But I realized something from that phantom just now.
俺の中で、かれんの存在が希薄になりつつある……そう思った時、不意に悲しみがこみ上: The existence of Karen within me is becoming faint... When I thought that, sadness suddenly welled up.
（ダメだ、これ以上考えちゃいけない……さっき、いつまでも引きずらないと決意したば: (No, I mustn't think about this anymore... Didn't I just decide to stop dragging this out forever!)
頭を振って、もたげかけた記憶を押さえつけると、ことさら足音を立てるように歩き始め: I shook my head, suppressed the memories that were starting to surface, and began walking, making a deliberate point of making noise with my footsteps.
その足音に気づいたのだろうか、アパートの前に居た女が顔を上げた。: Perhaps noticing those footsteps, the woman who was in front of the apartment looked up.
ためらいがちに声をかけて来たのは、紛れもなく望月恵美子その人だった。: The person calling out hesitantly was none other than Mochizuki Emiko herself.
さっきの幻と同じくらい有り得ない人物の登場に、俺は再び固まってしまった。: With the appearance of a person as impossible as the phantom from a moment ago, I froze once again.
宏治「も、望月さん……ですか？ どうして、ここに？」: Koji: “Is... is that you, Mochizuki-san? Why are you here?”
辛うじてそれだけを口にする。: I barely managed to say that much.
恵美子「ご、ごめんなさい……驚きますよね？ 突然、こんな……」: Emiko: “I-I’m sorry... I startled you, didn't I? Suddenly showing up like this...”

傍から見れば、俺たちは何の問題もないごく普通のカップルに見えるだろう。: From the outside, we would probably look like a perfectly normal couple without any problems.

おばさんナース「……でね、そしたらね……あら、ミヤマさん？ 聞いてるの？ ミヤマ: Old Nurse: “...So then, you see... oh, Miyama-san? Are you listening? Miyama-san!”

適当そのものの返事をして、その場を逃れる。: Giving a completely haphazard reply, I escaped the situation.
ちょっと話を聞いてなくても、今のように肯定しておけば、まず間違いは無い。: Even if I wasn't listening for a bit, as long as I agree like I just did, there's almost never a mistake.
おばさんナースの話は確かに長いものの、コミュニケーション自体は取りやすく、仕事が: Although the older nurses' stories were indeed long, the communication itself was easy, and the work was very easy to do.
どうやら俺は中年のおばさんに受けがいいらしく、どの科へ行っても大歓迎された。: It seems I'm popular with middle-aged women, and I was welcomed in every department I went to.
それに従って注文も増え、先週の成績は営業部内においてＮｏ．１だった。: In accordance with that, orders increased, and last week's performance was No. 1 within the sales department.
まるで大切なものと引き換えに、別なものを受け取ったような気分になる。: It feels like I've received something else in exchange for something precious.
会社に戻る途中の駅前で、携帯電話が鳴った。: On my way back to the company near the station, my mobile phone rang.
今ぐらいの時間になると、彼女は必ずといってよいほど携帯に電話を掛けてくる。: Around this time of day, she almost certainly calls my mobile.
うぬぼれた言い方をすれば、恵美子はこの俺に夢中らしい。: To put it in a conceited way, it seems Emiko is crazy about me.
でも、俺のほうが夢中にはなりきれていない。: But I haven't been able to become completely crazy about her.
心から抱きたい、と思えないのだ。: I can't feel a desire to hold her from my heart.
もちろん、彼女に魅力がないわけではない。: Of course, it's not that she lacks charm.
現に初めてウチのアパートに来た時には、あの問題の日の状況を思いだし、湧きあがって: In fact, when she first came to my apartment, I struggled to suppress the desire that welled up as I remembered the situation of that problematic day.
でもそれは……恵美子自身を相手に欲情した、というわけではなかったのかも知れない。: But that... might not have been lust directed at Emiko herself.
もちろん彼女に魅力がないわけではないし、一緒の時間を過ごすのは楽しかった。: Of course, it wasn't that she lacked charm, and spending time together was enjoyable.
ただ、あの日ばったり会ったかれんのことがどうしても頭から離れなかったのも、また事: However, it was also a fact that the Karen I had run into that day could not leave my mind no matter what.
（過ぎたことをクヨクヨしすぎだな……）: (I'm brooding too much over the past...)
恵美子「宏治さん、いま大丈夫？ 迷惑じゃない？」: Emiko: “Kouji-san, is now okay? Am I being a nuisance?”
恵美子が訪ねてきて以来、こうしてお互いを名前で呼び合うのも何回目だろうか。: Since Emiko visited, I wonder how many times we've called each other by our first names like this.
しかし、恵美子の名前を口に出すたびに感じる心の奥の疼きは、決して消えることはなか: However, the ache deep in my heart that I feel every time I speak Emiko's name never disappeared.
誘うのはいつも恵美子からだ。俺から誘ったことは、まだない。: The invitations always come from Emiko. I haven't invited her even once.
恵美子「また、宏治さんのアパートに行っていいかな？」: Emiko: “Is it okay if I go to your apartment again, Kouji-san?”
宏治「もちろん！ じゃあ、仕事を早く上げるようにするよ」: Koji: “Of course! Then, I'll make sure to finish work early.”
心持ち大げさに喜びの声を上げる。: I raised my voice in joy with a bit of exaggeration.
恵美子「それじゃ、８時くらいに行って大丈夫？」: Emiko: “In that case, is it okay if I go around 8 o'clock?”
宏治「うん、そうだね。メシは一緒に食べる？」: Koji: “Yeah, you're right. Shall we eat dinner together?”
恵美子「じゃあ、何か買っていこうか？」: Emiko: “Then, shall I buy something to bring over?”
宏治「そうだね、じゃ俺は飲み物を用意するから……」: Koji: “Yeah, then I'll prepare the drinks...”
あの日、恵美子がつぶやいた言葉……素直に彼女と付き合って、そして結ばれる……それ: The words Emiko muttered that day... honestly going out with her, and then being joined... that would be the natural flow.
恵美子を愛して、そして抱く……彼女もきっとそれを望んでいるに違いない。: Loving Emiko, and then holding her... she surely must want that too.
俺は期待と使命感が入り混じった、不思議な気持ちを抱いていた。: I harbored a strange feeling, a mix of expectation and a sense of duty.
いつもより遅い時間に会社に戻ると、もうほとんどの人はオフィスに残っていなかった。: When I returned to the company later than usual, almost no one was left in the office.
佐藤部長「おう、お疲れ。相変わらずがんばってんな！」: Director Sato: “Oh, good work. You're working as hard as ever!”
突然、背後から声をかけられてびっくりする。: Suddenly being called from behind, I was surprised.
佐藤部長は、俺が好成績続きのためか相変わらず機嫌がいい。: Manager Sato is in high spirits as usual, probably because of my continued good performance.
佐藤部長「どうだ？ 一杯付き合わんか？」: Director Sato: “How about it? Care to join me for a drink?”
宏治「すみません。せっかくですけど、まだまだやることがありますんで……」: Koji: “I'm sorry. Thank you for the offer, but I still have things to do...”
まだまだ残務もあるので、丁重に断る。: Since I still had remaining work, I declined politely.
佐藤部長「そうか、仕方がない。お前も相変わらず仕事熱心だな」: Director Sato: “I see, it can't be helped. You're as dedicated to your work as ever.”
まあこれも、会社人特有の社交辞令のようなものだ。: Well, this too is something of a social pleasantry unique to company people.
佐藤部長「あぁ、そう言えば……二、三週間前だったかな？ 総務の方に問い合わせがあ: Director Sato: “Ah, come to think of it... was it two or three weeks ago? It seems there was an inquiry to general affairs.”
一礼してデスクに向かおうとした時、部長が思い出したように言った。: As I bowed and headed toward my desk, the manager said it as if he'd just remembered.
佐藤部長「何だかお前の同窓生だか何だかが、お前の住所を知りたいって言って……」: Director Sato: “It seems like an old classmate of yours or someone was asking because they wanted to know your address...”
（同窓生？ いや……多分、恵美子だ……間違いない）: (A classmate? No... it's probably Emiko... no doubt about it)
佐藤部長「ん？ どうした？ 何かまずかったのか？」: Director Sato: “Hm? What's wrong? Was there something bad about it?”
訝しげな俺の表情に気付いたのか、部長は心配そうだ。: Noticing my suspicious expression, the manager looked worried.
宏治「あ、いえ、大学のサークルの後輩ですよ。ほら、就職活動の先輩訪問で……」: Koji: “Ah, no, it's a junior from my university club. You know, for an alumni visit for job hunting...”
口からでまかせを言って、その場を逃れる。: I said the first thing that came to my mind to escape the situation.
（……恵美子……偶然に俺を見かけたんじゃなかったのか？）: (...Emiko... didn't you see me by chance?)
俺は、ナース時代の恵美子のことを思い返していた。: I was thinking back on Emiko during her time as a nurse.
なぜかいつも、俺のそばにいた恵美子……そう、あの時も……。: Why was Emiko always by my side... yes, even at that time...
あの時、なぜ恵美子は外科病棟にいたのだろう？: At that time, why was Emiko in the surgical ward?
……婦長と沢田がカンファレンスルームでイイことをしていた、あの日あの時……それも: ...The head nurse and Sawada were doing that thing in the conference room, that day and that time... was that also a coincidence?
佐藤部長「どうした、宮坂？ やけに深刻な顔をして……何かトラブルか？ 俺でよけれ: Director Sato: “What's wrong, Miyasaka? You have a very serious look on your face... is it some trouble? If you like, I can give you advice.”
そう答えながらも、俺の考えは止まらない……。: While replying so, my thoughts wouldn't stop...
俺の家の側にあるという、恵美子の職場って……？ そういえば、どこでどんな仕事をし: Emiko's workplace that's supposed to be near my house...? Come to think of it, I haven't even heard where she works or what kind of job she's doing...
佐藤部長「そうか……ま、いつでも相談に乗るから。遠慮はするな」: Director Sato: “I see... well, I'm always available for a consultation. Don't hesitate.”
宏治「分かりました。ありがとうございます」: Koji: “I understand. Thank you very much.”
佐藤部長に会釈をしてデスクに向かうと、携帯を取り出して恵美子の番号をダイヤルする: After nodding to Manager Sato, I headed to my desk, took out my mobile, and dialed Emiko's number.
アナウンス「……お客さまがお掛けになった電話は、電波が届かないところにあるか、電: Announcement: “...The customer you have dialed is in an area without reception or the power is turned off.”
俺は苛立ちながら、再度ダイヤルをする。: While feeling irritated, I dialed again.
何となく、このままにしておいてはいけない気がした。: Somehow, I felt like I couldn't leave it like this.
恵美子「ああ……宏治さん？ 珍しいね……宏治さんから電話をくれるなんて……」: Emiko: “Ah... Kouji-san? That's rare... for you to call me...”
普段と変わりのないその声に、俺の意気込みがしぼんでくる。: At that voice, which was no different than usual, my enthusiasm withered.
恵美子「どうしたの？ 金曜日のこと……？」: Emiko: “What's wrong? Is it about Friday...?”

恵美子の声色が一瞬のうちに変わった。: Emiko's tone of voice changed in an instant.
恵美子「何か変だよ……今日の宏治さん……」: Emiko: “Something's strange... with you today, Kouji-san...”
恵美子「何を怒ってるの？ 口調が、怖いよ」: Emiko: “Why are you angry? Your tone is scary.”

恵美子「……上の人が来たから……切るね……金曜日に会った時でいいよね……」: Emiko: “...Someone from above came, so... I'll hang up... It's fine when we meet on Friday, right...?”
俺はムキになって、もう一度ダイヤルをする。: I became stubborn and dialed once again.
俺は舌打ちをして電話を切る。: I clicked my tongue and hung up the phone.
（何かを隠しているみたいだった……）: (It seemed like she was hiding something...)
恵美子に対する疑いが更に大きくなっていく。: The suspicion toward Emiko grows even larger.
（……金曜日まで待つしかない……か）: (I guess... I have no choice but to wait until Friday.)
電話を諦め、頭の中の不透明なもやもやを払いのけようと、懸命に今わかっている事実関: Giving up on calling, I desperately tried to organize the facts known so far to brush away the opaque fog in my head.
しかし、正直言って何が何だか分からなかった。: However, to be honest, I had no idea what was what.
不意に、かれんの笑顔が思い浮かぶ。: Suddenly, Karen's smile came to mind.
今の俺は、無性にあの笑顔が見たかった。: Right now, I desperately wanted to see that smile.
しかし今更どうするというのだ……あの日に戻ってやり直せるわけでもあるまい……。: But what was the point now... it's not like I could go back to that day and do it over...
余計な考えを払いのけ、目の前の書類整理に没頭しようとする……でも、どうしても集中: I tried to brush away unnecessary thoughts and immerse myself in organizing the documents in front of me... but I just couldn't concentrate...
宏治「部長！ 俺……やっぱり、飲みに連れて行ってください！」: Koji: “Manager! I... actually, please take me out for a drink!”
この状態に耐えられなくなって、帰ろうとしていた佐藤部長に声をかけてしまう。: Unable to bear this state, I called out to Manager Sato, who was about to go home.

机の上をさっと片付けて、部長の後を追う。: I quickly tidied my desk and followed after the manager.
例え酒の肴にどんな話をされようが、こんな状態よりは遥かにいい。: No matter what conversation he had over drinks, it was far better than being in this state.
それに今は、何もかも忘れてぶっ倒れるまで飲みたい気分だった……。: And right now, I felt like drinking until I forgot everything and collapsed...
佐藤部長「お、今日は早いな。お疲れさん」: Director Sato: “Oh, early today, I see. Good work.”
今日は、恵美子が家にやって来る約束の金曜日。: Today was Friday, the day I promised Emiko she could come over.
就業時間終了と同時に仕事を終え、一目散に家路につく。: I finished work the moment the business hours ended and made a beeline for home.
駅までの道を急ぎながら、先日の酒の席での佐藤部長の言葉を思い出す。: Hurrying toward the station, I recalled Manager Sato’s words from our recent drinks.
佐藤部長『いいか、宮坂。大人になると、妥協というものはどうしても必要になる』: Director Sato: “Listen, Miyasaka. When you become an adult, compromise becomes an absolute necessity.”

It even looks cool, so people tend to run in that direction while they’re young, but someone who can’t draw a firm line between ideal and reality can’t be called a grown man.”
佐藤部長『それは自己防衛でもあるが、周囲を傷つけないと言う社会性動物としての人間: Director Sato: “That’s an act of self-defense, but it’s also the basic stance of humans as social animals—to not hurt those around you.”
佐藤部長『妥協と言うと、格好悪いとか逃げ、というイメージもあるかも知れんが、自分: Director Sato: “Compromise might have an image of being uncool or an escape, but isn't it also quite uncool, depending on how you look at it, to not care at all how much you hurt those around you for the sake of your own ideals?”
部長の言葉を聞いているうちに、俺は憑き物が落ちたような感じがした。: While listening to the Manager’s words, I felt as if I’d been exorcised of a haunting spirit.
オヤジの説教なんてウザったい、などと酒の誘いを断るのではなく、もっと早く、部長の: Instead of declining his invitations thinking an old man’s preaching is annoying, I should have accepted his words as advice from an elder much sooner...
それを怠った為に、かれんという理想を追い続け、自分を傷つけ、会社に迷惑をかけ、担: Because I neglected that, I kept chasing the ideal of Karen, hurting myself, causing trouble for the company, causing trouble for my assigned hospital, and likely hurting Karen as well...
さらに、こんな状態のままで恵美子と付き合い続けると、彼女までも傷つけてしまうだろ: Furthermore, if I continued seeing Emiko in this state, I would surely end up hurting her too.
部長と話をするまでは、恵美子に聞きたいことが山ほどあった。: Until I talked with the Manager, there were mountains of things I wanted to ask Emiko.

真相を明らかにすることに、どれだけの意味があるというのだ。: What meaning would there be in bringing the truth to light?
今の俺のそばには、恵美子という心を寄せてくれる女性が居る。: Right now, by my side, there is a woman named Emiko who gives me her heart.
かれんのことを忘れて、恵美子と楽しく暮らす…………それでいいじゃないか。: Forget about Karen and live happily with Emiko... isn't that enough?
これも、一つの妥協なのだろうか？: Is this, too, a form of compromise?
（妥協か……でも、そんなことを言ったら恵美子に失礼だよな）: (Compromise, huh... but saying that would be rude to Emiko.)
そこまで考えて、俺は一人で苦笑する。: Thinking that far, I gave a lonely, bitter smile.
妥協というには、恵美子はあまりにも魅力的だ……そう、俺なんかにはもったいないくら: To call it a compromise, Emiko is far too attractive... yes, more than someone like me deserves...
今日はただ、恵美子と会えるのが楽しみだ。: Today, I’m simply looking forward to seeing her.
それで充分じゃないか？ それ以上に何を望む？: Isn't that sufficient? What more could I want?
（俺は、もっと早く大人になるべきだったんだろう……）: (I suppose I should have grown up much sooner...)
酒屋に寄って、いつもよりも少しだけ高価なワインを買う。: I stopped at the liquor store and bought a wine slightly more expensive than usual.
アパートへの道を歩き始めると、タイミングよく恵美子の俺を呼ぶ声が聞こえてきた。: As I started walking the path to the apartment, Emiko’s voice calling me was heard with perfect timing.
大きな買い物袋を二つ、両手に提げて駆けてくる姿を見て、それを『妥協』と呼ぶことへ: Seeing her running toward me with two large shopping bags hanging from her hands, the hesitation to call this ‘compromise’ welled up again.
けなげな恵美子に対して、心苦しくなると同時に、『俺は一体何様だ？』と自責の念にさ: Facing the devoted Emiko, I felt pained at the same time I was tormented by self-reproach, thinking, ‘Just who do I think I am?’
（俺なんて、まだまだガキだ。妥協だなんて、人を見下すような考えを持つ資格なんてな: (I’m still just a brat. I have no right to hold such look-down-your-nose thoughts like calling this a compromise...)
恵美子「よかったぁ、ちょうど一緒になりましたね」: Emiko: “I’m so glad, we happened to meet at just the right time.”
宏治「ああ、速攻で退社してきたんだ」: Koji: “Yeah, I left the office at high speed.”
息を切らして俺の前までやってきた恵美子に対して、笑顔を向ける。: I directed a smile at Emiko, who arrived before me out of breath.
今は小難しいことなど考えず、二人の時間を大切にすればいい……。: Right now, I shouldn’t think about difficult things and just cherish our time together...
恵美子「うふ……今日はお料理作ろうかと思って……たいしたものじゃないですけど」: Emiko: “Ufu... I thought I’d do some cooking today... it’s nothing special, though.”
宏治「本当？ それは楽しみだ」: Koji: “Really? I’m looking forward to that.”
俺の言葉に、嬉しそうに微笑む恵美子。いつもより濃いめの深紅のルージュが、艶かしさ: At my words, Emiko smiled happily. Her crimson rouge, darker than usual, emphasized her voluptuousness.
宏治「今日は、ゆっくりしていけばいいよ……電車なんか気にせずに」: Koji: “You should take your time today... without worrying about the trains.”
俺は自分の背中を少しだけ押すような思いで、こう言った。: I said this with the feeling of giving my own back a slight push.
頬を赤らめてうつむきつつも、恵美子の瞳が妖しく輝いたのを、俺は見落としはしなかっ: Though she looked down with blushing cheeks, I did not miss the bewitching light that sparkled in Emiko’s eyes.
宏治「ごちそうさま、すごくおいしかったよ」: Koji: “Thank you for the meal, it was delicious.”
恵美子の料理を食べた後の、正直な感想だ。: That was my honest impression after eating Emiko’s cooking.
キッチンで片付けをしながら、恵美子が嬉しそうに笑う。: While tidying up in the kitchen, Emiko laughed happily.
そういえば、恵美子の笑いに対する違和感も随分と薄くなった。: Come to think of it, that sense of incongruity I felt toward Emiko’s laughter has faded considerably.
比較対象となっていた、かれんの笑顔の記憶が薄れつつあるからなのか、それとも単に慣: Is it because the memory of Karen’s smile, which served as a point of comparison, is fading, or is it simply a matter of habituation...
あらゆる感情が日常的な習慣に埋没していく……このことに納得するのが、大人になるっ: Every emotion gets buried in daily habits... accepting this might be what it means to become an adult...

俺は必要以上に明るい感じで返事をする。: I replied with a tone that was brighter than necessary.
（恵美子との時間を楽しむ、俺はそう決めたんだ……）: (I decided I would enjoy my time with Emiko...)

宏治「そうだなぁ……今度リクエストさせてもらうよ」: Koji: “Let’s see... I’ll make a request next time then.”

片付けを終えた恵美子とワイングラスを傾けながら、他愛もない会話を続ける。: While tilting my wine glass with Emiko, who had finished tidying up, I continued our idle conversation.
だが、さっきまでと打って変わって、恵美子の態度が硬い。: But in a total reversal from a moment ago, Emiko’s attitude is stiff.

一抹の不安が頭の中をよぎる。: A shred of anxiety crossed my mind.

あのときに交わした『食事に行こう』っていう約束……覚えていてくれるのだろうか？: That promise to ‘go for a meal’ we made back then... I wonder if she remembers?
だめだ、発想がマイナスに向かっている。: No, my thoughts are heading in a negative direction.
（当たって砕けろ！ なるようになれ！ だ……無駄なことは考えないことだ）: (Give it a shot! Whatever will be, will be! ...It’s no use thinking of useless things.)
決心が鈍らないうちに外科へ行くか迷ったが、まずは高橋先生のところへ挨拶に行くこと: I hesitated about going to surgery before my determination wavered, but first I decided to go to Dr. Takahashi for a greeting.
やはり、順番は大切だ。元はといえば先生のお陰で、この病院の担当に戻れたのだから…: After all, order is important. In the first place, it’s thanks to the Doctor that I could return as the person in charge here...
それに……久しぶりにかれんと会うことに、正直、少し気後れをしているのかも知れない: And... to be honest, maybe I’m feeling a little intimidated about meeting Karen after a long time.
高橋先生の部屋のドアの前に立ち、ひとつ深呼吸をする。: Standing in front of Dr. Takahashi’s door, I took a deep breath.
宏治「高橋先生、ミヤマ産業・宮坂でございます。この度は、正式にこちらの担当に……: Koji: “Dr. Takahashi, this is Miyasaka from Miyama Industries. On this occasion, I have officially become the person in charge here...”
女の声「あら、お久しぶりね」: Woman's Voice: “My, it’s been a while.”
出し抜けに後ろから声をかけられて、マヌケな声を上げてしまった。: Being called from behind all of a sudden, I let out a stupid sound.
しかも、この声は……聞き覚えがある……でも、何でだ？: Moreover, this voice... I remember it... but why?
姿を現したのは長妻婦長だった。: The person who appeared was Head Nurse Nagatsuma.
そんな動揺を押し隠し、辛うじて挨拶の言葉を搾り出す。: Hiding my agitation, I barely squeezed out a greeting.
麗子「また、担当に戻られるそうね」: Reiko: “I hear you’re returning as the person in charge.”
俺の記憶のままの冷たい口調……。: The cold tone just as in my memory...
四年という月日の重たさを実感する。: I felt the weight of the four years.
麗子「ふふ、あなた、男っぷりがよくなったんじゃない？」: Reiko: “Fufu, haven't you become more of a man?”
かと思うと、こんなことを言う……。: When I thought that, she said something like this...
もちろん、社交辞令なのだろうが、どことなく艶かしさを感じるのは気のせいだろうか？: Of course, it must be a social pleasantry, but am I imagining that I feel a certain voluptuousness?
カンファレンスルームに呼び出されたことが、頭をよぎる……。: The memory of being called into the conference room crossed my mind...
やっぱり、俺はこの人が苦手だ。四年経っても苦手なのは変わらない。: As I thought, I’m not good with this person. Even after four years, that hasn't changed.
宏治「あ、ありがとうございます……た、高橋先生は……？」: Koji: “Th-thank you very much... is D-Dr. Takahashi here...?”
そんな考えを頭の隅に追いやりつつ、仕事の話に持って行こうと試みる。: Pushing such thoughts to a corner of my head, I tried to bring the conversation to work.
麗子「ちょっと席を外しているようね……私も、書類を持ってきたんだけど……」: Reiko: “It seems he’s stepped out for a moment... I also brought some documents, though...”
（高橋先生、不在なのか……よりによってこんな時に……）: (Dr. Takahashi is absent... of all times...)
こちらへ一歩踏み出した婦長の目が、妖しく輝く……俺には、獲物を狙う獣の目のように: The Head Nurse took a step toward me, and her eyes sparkled bewitchingly... to me, it felt like the eyes of a beast stalking prey.
ついつい俺は、婦長と沢田のカンファレンスルームでの情事を思い出す。: I inevitably recalled the Head Nurse’s affair with Sawada in the conference room.
多少老けたとはいえ、婦長は充分に魅力的な熟女特有の色香を放っていた。: Though she’d aged somewhat, the Head Nurse emitted a scent unique to a sufficiently attractive mature woman.
高橋「やあ、長妻婦長。すまんすまん、待たせたね……お、ミヤマさんも来てたのか」: Takahashi: “Ah, Head Nurse Nagatsuma. Sorry, sorry, kept you waiting... oh, Miyasaka-san was here too.”
俺が妄想と戦っていると、高橋先生が帰ってきた。: As I was fighting my delusions, Dr. Takahashi returned.
いつもは下品そのものとしか感じない声が、今はまるで福音のように響く。: The voice I usually felt was nothing but vulgar sounded like a gospel now.
婦長はその声が聞こえた瞬間、妖艶な色を消し、いつもの冷たい感じに戻っている。: the moment she heard that voice, the Head Nurse erased the bewitching color and returned to her usual cold feeling.
高橋「ごほん……ああ、長妻婦長は、この病院に復帰されたのだ。で、今後は欠員の出来: Takahashi: “Ahem... ah, Head Nurse Nagatsuma has returned to this hospital. So, from now on I’ve asked her to work as the Head Nurse in surgery where there was a vacancy...”
俺が意味ありげに視線を送ると、それに気づいたのか高橋先生が咳払いをしてから言った: As I sent a meaningful gaze, perhaps noticing it, Dr. Takahashi cleared his throat and said.
何か一波乱ありそうな、そんな予感がした。: I had a premonition that something turbulent was about to happen.
麗子「それでは高橋先生、これ、頼まれていた書類です。ミヤマさん、またお願いします: Reiko: “Well then, Dr. Takahashi, these are the documents you asked for. Miyasaka-san, I’ll be counting on you again.”
口ごもりながら返答する俺を一瞥すると、婦長は高橋先生に深々とお辞儀をして歩き去っ: Glancing at me as I replied while stammering, the Head Nurse bowed deeply to Dr. Takahashi and walked away.
高橋「ああ、すまんすまん。ま、ひとまず、入ってくれや」: Takahashi: “Ah, sorry, sorry. Well, for now, come in.”
高橋「いや、待たせて悪かったな」: Takahashi: “No, sorry for keeping you waiting.”
宏治「いえこちらこそ、この度はいろいろご迷惑をお掛けいたしまして……」: Koji: “No, same here, I caused various troubles on this occasion...”
高橋「いや、まあ、今後はこんなことのないように頼むよ」: Takahashi: “No, well, I’m counting on you to make sure such things don't happen in the future.”
宏治「わかりました。今後ともよろしくお願いいたします」: Koji: “I understand. I’ll be counting on you as well.”
もう一度、後頭部を高橋先生に見せつけるようなお辞儀をする。: Once again, I gave a bow that showed the back of my head to Dr. Takahashi.
高橋「ああ、長妻婦長……院長とヨリを戻したんだよ。まったくあきれたもんだが……ま: Takahashi: “Ah, Head Nurse Nagatsuma... she made up with the director. It’s quite appalling, but... well, the problematic other man has gone to Tokyo too...”
高橋「これで、万事昔のまんまってわけだ。ま、サカタ君もこうして戻って来たわけだし: Takahashi: “With this, everything is exactly as it was. Well, Sakata-kun has returned like this too. Faffaffa...”
確かに……名前を覚えないところもまったくそのままだ。: Certainly... the way he doesn't remember names is exactly as it was.
高橋「ま、ひとつまた、よろしく頼むよ」: Takahashi: “Well, I’ll be counting on you again.”
高橋「うむ。では、俺はこれから会議なんで、ここで失礼するよ」: Takahashi: “Umu. Well, I have a meeting now, so I’ll excuse myself here.”
その他の関係者に挨拶を済ませた俺は、かれんの居る外科ナースステーションへと向かっ: After finishing my greetings to other related parties, I headed toward the surgical nursing station where Karen was.
本当は、すぐにでもそうしたかった……だが、初日から仕事をおろそかにする訳にもいか: Truthfully, I wanted to do that immediately... but I suppose I couldn't neglect my work from the first day.
もう、あんな風に担当を替えられるのはごめんだ……四年という時間は、俺にとってあま: I don't want to have my assignment changed like that again... four years was far too long for me.
だが、この四年を戻すことはできなくても、その長い時の流れによってできた溝ならば、: But even if I can't return those four years, if it’s a rift created by that long flow of time, I can fill it with my own power.
その勇気が、俺の中で着実に根付いていた。: That courage was steadily taking root within me.
運命の流れが、全てかれんに向かっている……。そんな都合のいい解釈をしたって許され: The flow of fate is all heading toward Karen... I felt it would even be permitted to make such a convenient interpretation.
俺は、この瞬間を四年間も待ったのだから……。: Because I’d waited four whole years for this moment...
目の前のナースステーション……こうやってここに来るのを、どれだけ望んだことか……: The nursing station in front of me... how much I’d wanted to come here like this...
そんな感慨に浸りながら、周囲にいる白衣の女性をひとりひとり目で確認していく。: While immersing myself in such sentiment, I checked each of the women in white lab coats with my eyes.
だが、かれんの姿はない。: But Karen’s form is not there.
左右の病室をひとつひとつ覗きながら、足早に廊下を歩く……でも、どこにもかれんの姿: While peeking into each patient room on the left and right, I walked quickly down the corridor... but Karen’s form was nowhere.
いくつ目かの病室を覗いた後、少し冷静になって考えてみる。: After peeking into several patient rooms, I tried to think a bit more calmly.
（……まさか、今日は非番なんじゃ？ だとしたら、いくら探したって無駄だ……）: (...No way, could she be off duty today? If so, no matter how much I search, it’s useless...)
正式にこの病院担当になったのだから後日はあるし、これ以上ウロウロして、いきなり変: Since I’ve officially become the person in charge of this hospital, there will be later days, and it wouldn't be good to be thought of as a strange guy by wandering around any more.
どうしよう？ 今すぐにでも、かれんに会いたい気持ちだが……。: What should I do? I feel like meeting Karen right now, but...
とりあえず今日はここまでにして、俺は会社に戻ることにした。: For today, I decided to leave it at this and return to the company.
営業車に乗り、わざと窓を開けたまま走らせる。: I got into the company car and drove while leaving the window open on purpose.
復帰１日目、俺の気持ちはそれなりに満たされていた。: The first day of my return, my feelings were satisfied in their own way.
これから、かれんのいる場所に毎日でも来られる。そう思うと嬉しくて身震いさえしてし: From now on, I can come to the place where Karen is every day. Thinking that, I was so happy I even trembled.
今まで、どれだけかれんに会う瞬間を夢見てきたか……このぐらいで諦めるわけにはいか: Until now, how much I’d dreamed of the moment of meeting Karen... I can't give up at this point...
そう思った矢先、目の前の病室の扉が開いた。: Just as I thought that, the door of the patient room in front of me opened.
そして病室の中を振り返り、笑顔を向けているのは、紛れもなく……かれん！: And turning back into the patient room and directing a smile was none other than... Karen!
その動きが、スローモーションに見える……彼女の顔が、ゆっくりとこちらを向いて……: The movement looked like slow motion... her face slowly turned this way...
何度も練習してきた、四年ぶりの再会の挨拶を……。: The greeting for our reunion after four years, which I’d practiced many times...
だが、あまりの感激で何もできなかった……ただただ頭の中が真っ白になって……。: But with too much emotion, I couldn't do anything... my head just went completely white...
ふと気が付くと、かれんが目の前に居た。: When I suddenly noticed, Karen was in front of me.
かれん「どちらさまでしたっけ？ 関係者以外の方はご遠慮いただきたいのですが……」: Karen: “Who might you be? I’d like people other than those involved to refrain from coming in...”
本当に……俺のことを覚えていない？: Does she truly... not remember me?
何度も再会の瞬間を夢見ながら、ありえないと思いつつも頭をかすめていた最悪の展開…: While dreaming of the moment of reunion many times, the worst-case scenario that had crossed my mind even while thinking it was impossible...
まるで頭をガツンと殴られたような衝撃が走る……。: A shock ran through me as if my head had been struck with a club...
俺は、声も出せずに立ち尽くすしかなかった。: I could only stand there without being able to even make a sound.
同時にあの天使のような笑顔がこぼれる。: At the same time, that angel-like smile overflowed.
その瞬間、空気が一変する……世界が一変する。: At that moment, the atmosphere changed completely... the world changed completely.
そう、俺はその笑顔が見たくって……今まで、ずっと過ごしてきた……そんな風に素直に: Yes, I wanted to see that smile... until now, I’ve spent my time all along... I can say that honestly.
あまりの感動に返事も出来ず、ただ涙がこみ上げてくる。: Unable to reply with too much emotion, tears just welled up.
かれん「四年と二ヶ月ぶりだね。宮坂宏治君……」: Karen: “It’s been four years and two months. Kouji Miyasaka-kun...”
その言葉に頷きながら、この瞬間を一生忘れまい、と誓った。: While nodding at those words, I vowed never to forget this moment for the rest of my life.
変わらない……その笑顔も、温かな話し方も……かれん……俺の天使……。: Unchanging... that smile, that warm way of talking... Karen... my angel...
この美しいかれんの笑顔を一生保存しておきたいという思いで、心の中のシャッターを何: With the desire to preserve this beautiful smile of Karen’s forever, I clicked the shutter in my heart many times.
もう、何があってもこの笑顔を薄れさせたりはしない……。: I won't let this smile fade anymore, no matter what...
まるで、時間が止まっているかのように感じる。: It feels as if time has stopped.
彼女もまた、何も言葉を発することなく、ただその場で笑顔を浮かべたまま、俺の顔を見: She too said nothing, just remained there with a smile, staring at my face.
『何か言わないと』と思うのに、話すべき言葉が見つからない……。: I thought ‘I have to say something,’ but I couldn't find the words I should speak...
でも、その二人の間には確実に何か温かなものが通い始めていた。: But something warm was certainly starting to pass between the two of them.
二人の間に言葉なんていらない……そんな感覚を、俺はごく自然に受け入れることが出来: No words are needed between two people... I was able to accept that sensation quite naturally.
恵美子には決して起こり得なかった感覚……これが、恵美子とかれんの決定的な差だった: A sensation that could never happen with Emiko... maybe this was the decisive difference between Emiko and Karen.
言葉を発する代わりに、何度も思い描いたかれんの姿をしげしげと眺める。: Instead of speaking, I stared intently at Karen’s form, which I’d pictured many times.
以前に見た時は『痩せた』という印象だったが、改めて見ると『出る所が出て、引っ込む: When I saw her before, I had the impression she’d ‘gotten thinner,’ but looking at her again, I wonder if it’s more like ‘where it should be out is out, and where it should be in is in.’
それは、単に思い込みかも知れない……でも……。: That might just be my own assumption... but...
脳裏によこしまな妄想がよぎる。: A wicked delusion crossed my mind.
……四年といえば長い年月だし、こんな綺麗なかれんに何も無かったと思うのは都合がよ: ...Four years is a long time, and it’s far too convenient to think that nothing happened to such a beautiful Karen...
（男が放っておくわけがないよな……）: (There’s no way men would leave her alone...)
かれんのふくよかで白い肉体をもてあそぶ第三者の手を想像し、下半身が熱くなる。: Imagining the hands of a third party toying with Karen’s plump, white body, my lower body became hot.
（久々の再会だっていうのに、俺は一体何を考えているんだ……）: (Even though it’s a reunion after a long time, just what am I thinking...)
でも、心の暴走は留まることなく、それどころか益々エスカレートしていく。: But the runaway of my heart didn't stop, if anything it escalated more and more.
妄想の中、自らの手でそのカラダをまさぐるかれん……俺は夢中でカメラのシャッターを: In the delusion, Karen groping that body with my own hands... I continue to click the camera shutter frantically.
そしてやがて別の手が……俺はあくまで撮影者で、かれんに直接触れることはない……。: And soon another hand... I am merely the photographer, and I never touch Karen directly...
そう呼びかける声に我に返る。: I came to my senses at the voice calling me.
目を上げると、俺の卑猥な妄想など想像もしない様子で、かれんが天使のような笑みを浮: When I looked up, Karen wore an angel-like smile, appearing not to even imagine my obscene delusions.
館内放送「繰り返しお伝えいたします。ミヤマ産業様、ミヤマ産業様、お車の移動をお願: PA System: "Repeating the announcement. To those from Miyama Industries, those from Miyama Industries, please move your vehicle."
かれん「あれ……宮坂さんの車のことじゃない？」: Karen: “Wait... isn't that about your car, Miyasaka-san?”
かれんとの再会を考えるあまり、車を停める場所を間違えたのかも知れない。: Perhaps I’d mistaken the parking place because I was thinking too much about the reunion with Karen.
（そういえば、『お客様専用』って書いてなかったような……）: (Come to think of it, I don't think it said ‘For customers only’...)
そこまで考えて、完全に現実に戻る。: Thinking that far, I returned completely to reality.
宏治「すぐ移動させないと……じゃ、また……」: Koji: “I have to move it immediately... well then, see you later...”
そう言って、かれんに軽く会釈をする。: Saying so, I gave a brief bow to Karen.
心の中で続きを言って、踵を返す。: Saying the rest in my heart, I turned on my heel.
（今日は、これでいい……彼女が俺のことを覚えていてくれただけで充分だ……）: (For today, this is fine... it’s enough just that she remembered me...)
ここからまた、じっくりと俺たちの関係を築いていけばいい……。: From here, I just need to build our relationship steadily again...
病院の外に出てからは、ほぼ駆け足だ。: After going out of the hospital, I was almost running.
（恐らく『急を要する』ほどではないのだろうが、館内放送があったということは、俺が: (It probably wasn't to the point of ‘requiring urgency,’ but the fact there was an announcement means my parked car is being some kind of nuisance...)
そう思って、更に足を速めかけた時……。: Thinking so and starting to quicken my pace even more...
（背後から何か聞こえるような……）: (I feel like I hear something from behind...)
営業車の所まで戻ると、周囲には誰もいなかったものの、確かに他の来客の迷惑になりそ: When I returned to the company car, no one was around, but it was certainly a parking position that would be a nuisance to other visitors.
（せっかく戻って来れたんだから、もっとしっかりしなくちゃ……）: (Since I’ve finally been able to return, I have to be more firm...)
車に飛び乗り、わざと窓を開けたまま走らせる。: Jumping into the car, I drove while leaving the window open on purpose.
復帰１日目、俺の気持ちは充分に満たされていた。: The first day of my return, my feelings were fully satisfied.
かれんと同じ空気を吸い、同じ時間を過ごせたことで、胸がいっぱいになっていた。: Breathing the same air as Karen and spending the same time together, my heart was full.
俺は足を止め、後ろを振り向いた。: I stopped my feet and looked back.
振り向くと、息を切らしてかれんが走ってくる。: When I looked back, Karen was running toward me out of breath.
その姿を見た瞬間、車を移動させるなんてことは頭から消し飛んでしまった。: The moment I saw her, the thought of moving the car vanished from my head.
かれん「はぁっはぁっ……よかった、気づいてくれて。宮坂さん、歩くの速いんだもん」: Karen: “Haah, haah... I’m glad you noticed. You walk so fast, Miyasaka-san.”
宏治「あ、すいません。急いで車動かさないと、って思って……で、どうしました？ こ: Koji: “Ah, sorry. I thought I had to move the car quickly... so, what’s up? To chase me this far.”
かれん「うん、あのね……もう、忘れちゃったのかな？」: Karen: “Yeah, um... have you already forgotten?”
（かれんが言ってるのは……あの約束のことなのか？）: (Is what Karen is saying... about that promise?)
かれん「『今度、メシ食いに行こう』って」: Karen: “‘Let's go for a meal sometime.’”
その言葉に、ゆっくりと頷いて答える。: I replied with a slow nod to those words.
（やっぱりそうだ！ かれん、君も覚えていてくれたんだ……）: (As I thought! Karen, you remembered too...)
この四年間で出来た溝がまたひとつ埋まった気がして、心が明るくなる。: I felt another rift created over these four years was filled, and my heart brightened.
かれん「『好きなもの食べさせてやる。エビでもカニでもフグチリでも』って」: Karen: “‘I’ll let you eat whatever you like. Whether it’s shrimp or crab or pufferfish stew.’”
（ちょっと待て！ 俺は断じてそこまでは言ってない……）: (Wait a minute! I definitely didn't say that much...)
思わず、擬音つきでずっこけてしまう。: I reflexively stumbled with a sound effect.
かれん「おっ、なかなかノリがいいね～」: Karen: “Oh, you’re quite a good sport~”
（本当、こういうところも相変わらずだ……）: (Truly, even this part is the same as always...)
でも、この空気がとても心地よい……かれんの笑顔と柔らかな声、そして、十八番の寒い: But this atmosphere was very comfortable... Karen’s smile and soft voice, and her favorite lame jokes... things I’d wanted for four years were now right in front of me.
かれん「四年前の約束だけど……まだ、有効なのかな？」: Karen: “About the promise from four years ago... is it still valid?”
少し神妙な顔になると、彼女はそこで言葉を切って俺の答えを待っていた。: Assuming a slightly solemn face, she cut her words there and waited for my answer.
俺には、そのいたいけな瞳が少しだけ潤んでいるように見えた。: To me, those pitiful eyes looked a little moist.
宏治「もちろん！ ……どこへ行きます？ ごちそうしますよ」: Koji: “Of course! ...Where shall we go? It’s my treat.”
今度は、ハッキリと言葉に出して答える。: This time, I replied clearly with words.
その言葉に、かれんはパッと華やいだような笑顔を浮かべる。: At those words, Karen wore a smile as if she’d suddenly brightened up.
宏治「どこでも？ 遠慮なんてしなくて構わないのに？」: Koji: “Anywhere? You don't have to hold back, though?”
言ってから、『しまった』と思った。: The moment I said it, I thought ‘oops.’

宏治「それで、ちょっと……お待ちしているわけにもいかずに……」: Koji: “So, I... couldn't afford to wait...”
間違いない。俺が立ち聞きしていたのを婦長に見られたんだ。: There’s no mistake. The Head Nurse saw me eavesdropping.
（くッ……よりによって、なんで婦長に……）: ( Damn... why did it have to be the Head Nurse, of all people... )
気ばかり焦って、まともに喋れそうにない。: I’m just getting flustered and can barely speak properly.
となると、これ以上の言い逃れは逆効果だ。: If that's the case, any further excuses will be counterproductive.
（ここは黙ってやり過ごすしかないな……）: ( I have no choice but to keep quiet and get through this... )
宏治「あ、じゃあ、また明日お伺いしますんで、宜しくお願いいたします」: Koji: “Ah, well, I’ll visit again tomorrow then, thank you.”
頭を下げて立ち去ろうとした瞬間、長妻婦長の独り言のような呟きを耳にする。: The moment I bowed and tried to leave, I heard Head Nurse Nagatsuma mutter something to herself.
そう言ったような気がする。: I think that’s what she said.
その言葉をムリに聞かなかったことにして、急いで立ち去るしかなかった。: I had no choice but to pretend I didn't hear those words and leave in a hurry.
次の日の朝……俺はオフィスで悶々としていた。: The next morning... I was brooding in the office.
これから病院へ行かなくてはと思うと、昨日のことがどんどん気になってくる。: When I think that I have to go to the hospital, yesterday’s events keep bothering me more and more.
（高橋先生……あの部屋でいったいどんな風にして……）: ( Dr. Takahashi... just what kind of things was he doing in that room... )
声しか聞けなかった分だけ、余計にイメージが膨らんでしまう……。: Since I could only hear the voices, the image just grows all the more...
頭の中に広がる妄想を追い出し、出かける準備を始めた。: I chased away the delusions spreading in my head and began preparing to head out.
苦笑しながらカバンに資料を突っ込む。: I shove materials into my bag with a wry smile.
振り向くと、直属の上司である佐藤部長が立っていた。: Looking back, my direct superior, Manager Sato, was standing there.
すると佐藤部長が会議室の方を指差す。: Then Manager Sato points toward the meeting room.
突然の呼び出しに不安になりつつも、会議室に向かう部長のあとを黙ってついて行った。: While becoming anxious at the sudden summons, I silently followed the manager as he headed toward the meeting room.
タバコの匂いの充満する会議室へと入り、ドアを閉める。: Entering the smoke-filled meeting room, I closed the door.
（一体、どうしたっていうんだ……）: ( What on earth is going on... )
部長の顔を見て、不安がますます募ってきた。: Looking at the manager's face, my anxiety grew even further.
いつも温厚なオヤジなのだが、その笑顔が今は見られない。: He’s usually a gentle old guy, but that smile was nowhere to be seen now.
佐藤部長「……お前、一体何をやらかしたんだ？」: Director Sato: “...What on earth did you pull?”
佐藤部長「すごい剣幕だったぞ……院長が自ら電話してきた……」: Director Sato: “He was in a real fury... the Director called me personally...”
長妻婦長の訝しげな顔が脳裏に蘇る。: Head Nurse Nagatsuma's suspicious face flashes through my mind.
（きっと院長にチクったんだな……何もしていないってのに……）: ( She must have ratted me out to the Director... even though I didn't do anything... )
佐藤部長「すぐに、担当を替えろと。そうでないとウチとの取引を全てやめると言ってき: Director Sato: “He told me to change the person in charge immediately. Otherwise, he’d stop all business with us...”
佐藤部長「理由は聞いても話してくれなかったが、大体察しはつく……」: Director Sato: “He wouldn't tell me why even when I asked, but I can pretty much guess...”
（そうだ……何もしちゃいない……）: ( That's right... I didn't do a thing... )
佐藤部長「お前くらいの年の男なら仕方がないことだと思うところもある……だが……」: Director Sato: “I think it’s somewhat inevitable for a man of your age... but...”
佐藤部長「いや、お前の言いたいことはわかるが、ナースに手をつけるのはまずい……」: Director Sato: “No, I understand what you want to say, but laying a hand on the nurses is bad...”
佐藤部長「あそこの病院は特に若くて可愛いナースが多いって評判だ……」: Director Sato: “That hospital has a reputation for having especially young and cute nurses...”
佐藤部長「お前の前の担当も、それでクビになっている……でもな、宮坂……俺はお前に: Director Sato: “Your predecessor was fired for the same thing... but, Miyasaka... I have high expectations for you. You remind me of myself when I first joined the company...”
真剣な眼差しで語りつづける部長に、俺は口を挟めなかった。: I couldn't interrupt the manager as he continued to speak with a serious gaze.
佐藤部長「……ということで、担当先を替わってもらう」: Director Sato: “...So, I’m going to have you change your assignment.”
いくら部長に本当のことを言ったところで、どうなるとも思えなかった。『お客様のおっ: No matter how much I told the manager the truth, I didn't think it would lead to anything. It’s that 'what the customer says is the truth' thing...
佐藤部長「そこは……まあ、看護婦がおばさんばっかりの病院だ。だから、まず間違いは: Director Sato: “The place is... well, it’s a hospital where the nurses are all old ladies. So, surely no mistakes will happen...”
佐藤部長「早速、今日から新しい病院へ行ってくれ。後のことは俺が処理しておくから…: Director Sato: “Go to the new hospital starting today. I’ll handle the rest... I’m counting on you...”
うつむく俺を残して、佐藤部長は会議室を出ていった。: Leaving me with my head hung low, Manager Sato left the meeting room.
この汚名はずっと付いてまわる。この業界はそういうところだ……。: This stigma will follow me around forever. That’s just how this industry is...
（畜生……運が悪かったな……）: ( Damn... I had some bad luck... )
理不尽な処分への腹立ちはあったが、同時に自分の無力さを恨めしく感じた。: There was anger at the irrational punishment, but at the same time, I felt wretchedly helpless.
（もう、潮時かな……）: ( Maybe it’s time to call it quits... )
退職の二文字が頭をよぎる。: The thought of resigning crossed my mind.
何もかもゼロにしたい……やり直したい気持ちで俺の心はいっぱいになっていた。: I want to wipe everything back to zero... my heart was filled with the desire to start over.
いや、やはりここで助け舟を出すのは危険だ。: No, after all, it’s dangerous to offer help here.
高橋先生の恨みを買ってはマズイ。: It would be bad to earn Dr. Takahashi's grudge.
（結構、根に持つタイプだからな……出直すのが一番だ）: ( He's the type to hold a grudge, after all... starting over is for the best. )
高橋先生との面会を諦め、俺はきびすを返す。: Giving up on seeing Dr. Takahashi, I turn on my heel.
（もしや、俺の動きをじっと見ていたのでは……）: ( Could it be she was watching my movements... )
（いや……考えすぎってこともあるさ……今きたのかも知れないじゃないか）: ( No... maybe I’m overthinking it... she might have just arrived. )
動揺をひた隠しにして、作り笑いを向ける。: Hiding my agitation, I give her a forced smile.
間違いない。俺が覗いていたのを婦長に見られたんだ。: There’s no mistake. The Head Nurse saw me peeping.
（大変なことになってしまった……）: ( This has turned into a disaster... )
（婦長の最後の言葉……空耳だったらいいんだが……）: ( The Head Nurse's last words... I hope they were just my imagination... )
（なるようにしかならないか……）: ( Well, what will be, will be... )
半ば仕方ないといった気持ちで、出かける準備をする。: Feeling half-resigned, I prepare to head out.
（昨日のことか……いや、いくらなんでも……）: ( Is it about yesterday... no, surely not... )
（きっと院長にチクったんだな……）: ( She must have ratted me out to the Director... )
（まさか覗きをしたとは言えない……）: ( There’s no way I can say I was peeping... )
（でも……これ以上のことは言えない……）: ( But... I can't say anything more than that... )
佐藤部長「もう、今日から新しい病院へ行ってくれ。後のことは俺が処理しておくから。: Director Sato: “Go to the new hospital starting today. I’ll handle the rest. I’m counting on you...”
うかつに部屋を覗いてしまった自分の行動が悔やまれる。: I regret my own action of carelessly peeping into the room.
（よし、そうと決まれば強行突破だ！）: (Alright, if that's how it's going to be, I'll force my way through!)
俺はその一団の中を分けいり、中心部へと入っていった。: I pushed through the group and made my way to the center.
その男が立ち止まり、視線を向けてきた。: The man stopped and turned his gaze toward me.
クールな声だった。だがその表情には、俺を値踏みするような色が浮かんでいる。: His voice was cool. But in his expression, there was a look as if he were sizing me up.
宏治「ミヤマ産業・宮坂と申します！ こちらの病院を担当させていただいております！: Koji: “I'm Miyasaka from Miyama Industries! I'm in charge of this hospital!”
医師「な、何だ君は！ いきなり失敬じゃないか！」: Doctor: “Wh-what are you doing! That's incredibly rude of you!”
当たって砕けろの精神で名刺を突きだした俺に、取り巻きの一人が詰め寄って来る。: One of his followers closed in on me as I thrust out my business card with a do-or-die spirit.
沢田「いいんだ、松本君……ミヤマ産業の宮坂さん、だね？」: Sawada: “It's fine, Matsumoto-kun... You're Mr. Miyasaka from Miyama Industries, right?”
沢田「１５分後に、カンファレンスルームに来たまえ。話を聞こう」: Sawada: “Come to the conference room in fifteen minutes. I'll hear what you have to say.”
沢田助教授はにこりともせずにくるりと身を翻して、その場を去っていった。: Without so much as a smile, Assistant Professor Sawada spun around and left the spot.
もちろん取り巻きの連中も慌てて、後を追っていった。: Naturally, his followers hurried to chase after him.
（ああ、うまくいった……やってみるもんだな……）: (Ah, it worked... It was worth a try...)
緊張から解かれて一息つく。: Relieved of the tension, I took a breath.
（……さて、一発、大きな注文でも決めてやるか！）: (...Now, let's go land a massive order!)
深呼吸をして、会社案内と今度の新製品のカタログを用意する。: Taking a deep breath, I prepared the company brochure and the catalog for the upcoming new products.
相手は天才外科医の名をほしいままにしている大物だ。: The opponent is a big shot who enjoys the reputation of being a genius surgeon.
（よっぽどうまくやらないと、軽くあしらわれて『はい、さようなら』だ。そうはいくも: (If I don't play this perfectly, he'll just brush me off with a 'Yes, goodbye.' I won't let that happen.)
俺の中の野心がめらめらと炎を上げる。: The ambition inside me flared up like a blazing fire.
出世した俺を尊敬の眼差しの目で見つめる高梨さんを想像してみた……悪くないと感じた: I imagined Takanashi-san looking at me with eyes of respect after my promotion... I felt it wasn't a bad thought at all.
俺の妄想を断ち切るかのように、沢田助教授がノックもせずに入ってくる。: As if cutting through my delusions, Assistant Professor Sawada entered without knocking.
その瞬間、心臓が早鐘のように鳴り響く。: In that instant, my heart pounded like a warning bell.
沢田「適当に座ってくれたまえ、宮坂さん」: Sawada: “Please, take a seat, Mr. Miyasaka.”
高橋先生と違って一発で俺の名を覚えてくれたようだ。: Unlike Dr. Takahashi, it seemed he had remembered my name on the first try.
宏治「は、はじめまして……この度は……」: Koji: “I-It’s a pleasure to meet you... on this occasion...”
沢田「宮坂さん……この際、無駄な前置きは無しにしよう。君も僕も時間がない……そう: Sawada: “Mr. Miyasaka... let's skip the useless introductions this time. Both you and I are short on time... wouldn't you agree?”
いきなり正論をぶつけられて、俺は返事をしただけで言葉が続けられなくなってしまう。: Hit suddenly with such sound logic, I could do nothing but answer, unable to continue my words.
沢田「僕が望んでいることはひとつだ。ミヤマ産業の親会社であるガイバー製薬……」: Sawada: “There is only one thing I desire. Guyver Pharmaceuticals, the parent company of Miyama Industries...”
突然発せられたその名前に、俺はぽかんと口を開けて彼の顔を見つめるばかりだった。: At that name suddenly uttered, I could only stare at his face with my mouth agape.
沢田「君も知っている通り、ガイバーは外資が８０％以上、もちろん経営陣もほとんどが: Sawada: “As you know, Guyver is over 80% foreign-owned, and naturally, almost all of the management are Americans.”
沢田「本国のガイバーには顧問制度というのがあって、それなりに名のある医師がその職: Sawada: “Guyver in their home country has a consultant system, and physicians of a certain renown hold those positions.”
沢田「日本においても医師による顧問制度を開始しようとの動きがあるようだ。もちろん: Sawada: “It seems there is a movement to start a physician-based consultant system in Japan as well. Of course, as you know...”
沢田「僕はその初代顧問になりたいと考えている。それなりのステイタスと話題性が生じ: Sawada: “I want to become that first consultant. Because I believe it will bring a certain status and prestige. Therefore...”
俺はその迫力に圧倒され、言葉を発することも出来ない。: Overwhelmed by his intensity, I couldn't even utter a word.
（やはり切れ者かも知れない……）: (He really might be a sharp one...)
沢田「僕は君をパイプとして働きかけをしたい。しかも秘密裏にだ」: Sawada: “I want to use you as a pipe to exert influence. And secretly, at that.”
沢田「そうだ。医師の世界はとても狭い世界だし、まだまだ封建的な風潮が残っている。: Sawada: “Correct. The world of physicians is a very narrow one, and a feudalistic atmosphere still remains. I cannot move directly...”
沢田「君は僕の言う通りに動いてくれればいいわけだ……もちろん、ただでとは言わない: Sawada: “You just have to move exactly as I say... Of course, I'm not saying I'll ask this for free...”
（一体何を言い出すんだろう……）: (Just what on earth is he going to say next...)
まるで遠い世界の出来事のような話を口にする沢田助教授の姿を見ながら、ぼんやりとそ: While watching Assistant Professor Sawada speak of things that sounded like events from a distant world, I vaguely thought such things.
沢田「現在、当院の外科において、君のところのライバル会社であるタケモト医療器との: Sawada: “Currently, in the surgery department of this hospital, the transaction volume with your rival, Takemoto Medical Instruments, is roughly double that of Miyama Industries.”
沢田「僕の権限で、その取引額の約７５％を君のところへ移そう……少なくとも、君の営: Sawada: “By my authority, I shall move approximately 75% of those transactions to your company... at the very least, your sales performance will skyrocket...”
沢田「もちろん、君個人にも何らかのメリットを与えるつもりだ。どうだろうか？ 悪い: Sawada: “Of course, I intend to provide some benefit to you personally as well. How about it? It shouldn't be a bad deal.”
沢田「まさか、君もつまらない一営業マンとして終わるつもりではあるまい？」: Sawada: “Surely, you don't intend to end your career as just a trivial salesman, do you?”
その一言が俺の胸にヒットした。: Those words hit me right in the chest.
（もちろん、俺だってこのままで終わるつもりなどない……）: (Of course, I don't intend to end up like this either...)
宏治「も、もちろんです！ や、やらせてください。助教授にご協力、させていただきま: Koji: “O-of course! P-please let me do it. I'll cooperate with you, Assistant Professor...”
どもりながらもハッキリ頷いた俺に、沢田助教授が初めて笑顔を見せる。: Even while stammering, I nodded clearly, and Assistant Professor Sawada showed a smile for the first time.
沢田「そうか、ありがとう。それでは、この話は君と僕の間だけでの話だ」: Sawada: “I see, thank you. Well then, this conversation stays strictly between you and me.”
沢田「上司にも話さないで、あくまで単独で行動するんだ。約束してくれるかい？」: Sawada: “Don't speak to your superior either; act entirely on your own. Can you promise me that?”
沢田「じゃあ、契約金として、手始めに今月からタケモトの分を少し移すとしよう」: Sawada: “Then, as a signing bonus, let's start by moving a portion of Takemoto's share starting this month.”
沢田「まず、このリストの物品の見積もりをもってきてくれないか？それと……」: Sawada: “First, could you bring me a quote for the items on this list? And also...”
沢田助教授の口ぶりは穏やかだが、目は笑ってはいない。: Assistant Professor Sawada's tone was calm, but his eyes were not smiling.
沢田「……君個人へのメリットだが、そちらも近々準備しよう」: Sawada: “...Regarding the benefit to you personally, I'll prepare that soon as well.”
淡々と続く沢田助教授の言葉に、俺の胸は震えていた。: My chest trembled at Assistant Professor Sawada's flatly delivered words.
（まるで悪魔と取引したような気分だな……）: (It feels as if I've made a deal with the devil...)
自らの魂と引き換えに得るものは、いったいどれだけのものだろう。: I wonder just how much I'll gain in exchange for my own soul.
沢田助教授に協力することは、俺のためになる……だがしかし、彼女はどう思うだろう…: Cooperating with Assistant Professor Sawada will be for my sake... but I wonder what she would think...
（いや……まずは富と力だ。それを手に入れれば何とでもなる）: (No... wealth and power come first. Once I have those, I can deal with anything.)
沢田「改めてこちらから連絡するまで、動かないで欲しい。それと、重ねて言うが……」: Sawada: “Don't move until you hear from me again. And, let me repeat...”
また、厳しい顔に戻った沢田助教授が少し低い声で言った。: Returning to a stern expression, Assistant Professor Sawada said in a slightly low voice.
沢田「今日のことは口外無用だ」: Sawada: “What happened today must not be breathed to a soul.”
沢田「じゃ、そう言うことで。よろしく頼むよ」: Sawada: “Well, then that's that. I'm counting on you.”
踵を返して立ち去る沢田助教授の後ろ姿を、頭を下げて見送る。: Bowing, I watched the back of Assistant Professor Sawada as he turned on his heel and left.
（……さて、うまくやるぞ）: (...Now, I'm going to play this well.)
俺は覚悟を決めると、心の中でそう呟いた。: Once I had steeled my resolve, I muttered that to myself in my heart.
その日から程なくして、約束通り沢田助教授はすぐに他社の発注分を俺に回してきた。: Not long after that day, just as promised, Assistant Professor Sawada immediately started directing orders from other companies to me.
不自然に見えないように、徐々に、徐々に……それにつれ、俺の営業成績は急速にあがり: Gradually, gradually, so it wouldn't look unnatural... and along with it, my sales performance began to rise rapidly.
もちろん、上司を含めた会社の連中には、多少の疑惑の念を抱いた者も居たかも知れない: Naturally, there might have been some among the company staff, including my superior, who harbored some suspicion.
しかし、この業界においてはこんな事は日常茶飯事なのか、いつのまにか誰も気を留めな: However, perhaps such things are commonplace in this industry; before long, no one paid it any mind.
医師「……と、いうことでよろしく頼むよ」: Doctor: “...And so, I'm counting on you.”
宏治「わかりました。では、後ほどそちらへお伺いいたします」: Koji: “Understood. I'll visit you later then.”
電話を切って溜息をつく。: I sighed as I hung up the phone.
沢田助教授から直接指示が来ることは無い……今のように、全て別の医師を通して指示が: Instructions never come directly from Assistant Professor Sawada... just like now, instructions are handed down through entirely different physicians... and he’s careful enough to use a different physician every time.
彼は、実に巧妙に事を運んでいる……この調子だと、院内に息のかかった人間は相当数居: He's handling things quite cleverly... at this rate, there must be a considerable number of people under his influence within the hospital.
（さて、高橋先生のところへ顔を出さないとな……）: (Now, I have to go show my face at Dr. Takahashi's place...)
正直言って気持ちが重い……高橋先生が、俺と沢田助教授の関係を勘ぐっている様子だか: To be honest, my heart is heavy... because Dr. Takahashi seems to be suspicious of the relationship between me and Assistant Professor Sawada.
最近は、高梨さんと顔を会わせることさえ何となく抵抗を感じる。: Lately, I feel a certain resistance even to meeting Takanashi-san face to face.
（いや、もう少しの辛抱だろう……）: (No, I'm sure it's just a little more patience...)
富、名誉、力……約束された将来が待っているはずだ……。: Wealth, honor, power... a promised future should be waiting...
高橋「なぁ……最近、何だかおかしくないか？」: Takahashi: “Hey... hasn't something been strange lately?”
浮かない気分のまま顔を出した俺に対し、開口一番に高橋先生が切りだす。: Dr. Takahashi cut to the chase as the first thing he said to me, as I showed my face with a gloomy mood.
高橋「まるで価格操作でもされたかのように、タケモトの価格の一円マイナスで決まって: Takahashi: “It's as if the prices were manipulated; things are being decided at exactly one yen less than Takemoto's price...”
珍しく書類の金額まで持ち出した高橋先生に、俺は思いっきりすっトボケた口調で答える: I answered with an entirely playing-dumb tone to Dr. Takahashi, who unusually brought up the figures in the documents.
高橋「この物品は外科管轄だから、俺は何も言えないがな……だが、ミヤマさん」: Takahashi: “Since these items are under the jurisdiction of surgery, I can't say anything... But, Miyama-san.”
高橋「あんたに沢田を紹介してからだよな……」: Takahashi: “It started after I introduced Sawada to you, didn't it...”

俺はその言葉に曖昧に頷いたまま、ただひくひくと息づく恵美子の蜜壷の感触を味わうば: While nodding vaguely at those words, I simply savored the sensation of Emiko’s honey pot as it pulsated.
（確かに……恵美子を手放すのはもったいないな……）: (Certainly... it would be a waste to let go of Emiko...)
射精直後の何となく冷めていく頭で、俺はそう考えていた。: With my head gradually cooling down immediately after ejaculation, that’s what I was thinking.
宏治「ああ……わかった……じゃあ、あとで……」: Koji: “Yeah... I get it... well, later then...”
恵美子からの電話を切った後で、俺は溜息をつく。: After hanging up the phone from Emiko, I let out a sigh.
結局、恵美子との関係もこれはこれでいいか、と思うようになっていた。: In the end, I had come to think that this kind of relationship with Emiko was fine.
相変わらずこの病院でおばさんナースの相手をしながら、単調な日々が過ぎていっている: The monotonous days continue to pass while I deal with the older nurses at this hospital as usual...
（こうして……かれんのことも忘れてしまうんだろうな……）: (I’ll probably... end up forgetting about Karen too, like this...)
そう思うと少しだけ寂しくもなるけど、仕方がない。: Thinking that makes me a little lonely, but it can't be helped.
俺は正直言って、こんな単調な日々の暮らしの中に、心地よさすら感じ始めていた。: To be honest, I was even starting to feel a sense of comfort in this monotonous daily life.
空を見上げて深呼吸をすると、全てがどうでもよく思えてくる。: When I look up at the sky and take a deep breath, everything starts to seem like it doesn't matter.
よく晴れた日曜日。俺は恵美子と待ち合わせをしていた。: A clear, sunny Sunday. I was waiting for Emiko.
約束の時間よりもかなり早く来てしまったのは、緊張のためだろうか……。: Did I arrive much earlier than the appointed time because of my nerves...?
思わぬ方向から恵美子が現れたので、少しびっくりしてしまう。: Emiko appeared from an unexpected direction, so it startled me a little.
宏治「いや、俺も今きたばっかりだし……」: Koji: “No, I just got here myself...”
とりあえず嘘を言っておく。早く来すぎたのを知られるのも、何となく気恥ずかしかった: I tell a lie for the time being. It was somehow embarrassing to let her know I’d come too early.
通り過ぎていく男が、恵美子に軽く視線を向けたりするのに気づいた。確かに恵美子は視: I noticed a man passing by glance briefly at Emiko. I truly think she’s a beautiful woman, enough to draw eyes.
容姿だけでなく、恵美子の醸し出す雰囲気も関係しているかも知れない。どこか晴れ晴れ: It might be due to the atmosphere she gives off, not just her looks. She has a somewhat radiant expression.
少し前までのような、笑みの中にも何か心の底では安心していないような不安定さが、今: I could no longer feel that instability she had until recently—that sense of not being at ease deep down even while smiling.
宏治「いや、ちょっと考え事してただけだよ」: Koji: “No, I was just thinking a little.”
できる限り明るい笑顔を作って、恵美子に手を伸ばす。: I force the brightest smile I can and reach out my hand to Emiko.
恵美子の顔つきに影が差し、俺は妙に胸がざわついた。: A shadow cast over Emiko’s face, and my heart stirred with a strange unease.
言いよどんでしまった自分を情けなく感じる。だがこれはあくまでためらいではなく、緊: I feel pathetic for hesitating. But this isn't exactly hesitation; it’s just nerves.
（これでいいんだ。自分で決めたことじゃないか）: (This is fine. I decided this myself, didn't I?)
恵美子「今なら、まだやり直せるけど……やっぱり宏治の気持ちが一番大事だから……」: Emiko: “We could still start over now... Kouji, your feelings are the most important thing...”
宏治「やり直すなんて言うなよ……」: Koji: “Don’t say things like starting over...”
恵美子「ううん……そうじゃない、そうじゃないけど……」: Emiko: “No... it’s not that, it’s not that, but...”
俺は恵美子の瞳をじっと見つめる。てっきり余裕や自信というものを身に付けたかと思っ: I stare straight into Emiko’s eyes. I thought she’d gained composure and confidence, but it wasn't that simple after all.
長い間、恵美子のことを大事にしてやれなかった。それが容易くチャラになると思うなん: I hadn't cherished Emiko for a long time. Thinking that would easily be wiped clean... I’m still a novice.
今度はしくじらないように、深呼吸をしてから口を開く。: So as not to screw up this time, I take a deep breath before opening my mouth.
宏治「……俺にできればいいな、お義父さんの仕事」: Koji: “...I hope I can do it well, your father’s work.”
瞳に大粒の涙を浮かべ、嗚咽まじりの声になる恵美子。: Large tears well up in her eyes, and Emiko’s voice becomes mixed with sobs.
俺はいたわるように、そっと恵美子の肩に手をかける。: I gently place my hand on Emiko’s shoulder, as if to comfort her.
恵美子「ごめんね……こんなところで……」: Emiko: “I’m sorry... in a place like this...”
宏治「いいよ、気にするなよ」: Koji: “It’s fine, don’t worry about it.”
恵美子「……私が目を赤くしてたら……宏治がお父さんに怒られるよね……」: Emiko: “...If my eyes are red... your father will get mad at you, Kouji...”
泣きながら機嫌のいい声になる恵美子。: Still crying, Emiko’s voice turns cheerful.
俺たちはこれから、恵美子の実家へ挨拶に行くところだ。: From here, we’re going to Emiko’s childhood home to give our greetings.
恵美子の実家は代々続いている老舗の酒屋で、彼女は三人姉妹の長女のため、家業を継げ: Emiko’s family home is a long-established liquor store that’s continued for generations, and as the eldest of three sisters, she was expected to marry a groom who could take over the business.
これから恵美子と毎日を過ごすことになる。残念がる佐藤部長には申し訳なかったが、俺: From now on, I’ll be spending every day with Emiko. I felt bad for Manager Sato, who expressed his regret, but I chose to quit the company and become the master of a liquor store.
俺はどこか自分に言い聞かせるかのようにそう思っていた。: I thought that as if trying to convince myself.
（後悔は全くない、といえば嘘かも知れない……でも……）: (It might be a lie to say I have no regrets at all... but...)
ここらで心と暮らしの安定を求めたっていいだろう……。: It’s probably fine to seek stability for my heart and my life at this point...
恵美子の手をとり、俺たちは並んで歩き出す。: Taking Emiko’s hand, we begin to walk side-by-side.
この辺りで暮らすこともなくなると思うと、ふと今までの思い出が頭をよぎる。: Thinking that I won’t be living around here anymore, my past memories suddenly cross my mind.
それは辞めた会社のことだったり、あの病院のことだったり……そう考えるうちに、どう: Things like the company I quit, or that hospital... and while thinking of those, one woman inevitably comes to mind.
あの笑顔に満ちた姿に、胸の奥の方が少しだけ痛む。: Looking at her image full of smiles, the depths of my chest ache just a little.
恵美子「ううん……ただ、呼んでみたかったの……」: Emiko: “Nothing... I just felt like calling your name...”
そう言うと、俺の腕にぎゅっとしがみついてくる。: Saying that, she clings tightly to my arm.
（何を考えてたかなんて、絶対言えないな……）: (I could never tell her what I was thinking...)
（いやもう……忘れよう……その方がいいんだ）: (No, enough... I’ll forget... that’s for the best.)
そう心の中で決めて、恵美子の手を強く握り締める。: Deciding that in my heart, I squeeze Emiko’s hand firmly.

（そうだ……これでいいんだ……）: (That’s right... this is for the best...)
まるで俺は自分自身に諭すように呟いた。: I muttered as if lecturing myself.
そして、これから過ごす町のこと……新しく家族になる人々のことを考えるようにした。: And then, I tried to think about the town where I’d be living... and the people who would become my new family.
（よし！ また、明日から通いづめだな……）: (Alright! Starting tomorrow, I’ll be heading there every day again...)
また、あの頃の雰囲気を早く取り戻して……。: I need to quickly get back that atmosphere from those days...
（覚えているかな？ あの約束……）: (I wonder if she remembers? That promise...)

今度こそ、じっくりと確実にいくんだ……。: This time, I’ll go slowly and surely...
部長の顔がどこか強張っている。: The Manager’s face was somewhat stiff.
（いやな予感がするな……）: (I have a bad feeling about this...)
佐藤部長「……小林がさっき辞表を書いてきた。まったく、最近の若いやつは……」: Director Sato: “...Kobayashi just wrote his resignation. Honestly, these young kids today...”

宏治「え？ 処分が不服だったってことですか？」: Koji: “Eh? Does that mean he was dissatisfied with the disciplinary action?”
佐藤部長「……と、いうか……まったく反省の色がない」: Director Sato: “...Rather... he showed no sign of remorse whatsoever.”

そう、俺は彼女に認められるような男になりたい……。: Yes, I want to become the kind of man she recognizes...

かれん「宮坂さん……最近、忙しそうで……」: Karen: “Miyasaka-san... you’ve seemed so busy lately...”

少し不満そうな表情を浮かべるかれん。思えば最近は、彼女のイマイチなギャグにもうま: Karen wore a slightly dissatisfied expression. Thinking back, I haven't even been able to properly banter with her mediocre jokes lately.
宏治「ごめんなさい……時間がないんで、また……」: Koji: “I’m sorry... I don’t have time, so, later...”

かれんは一瞬戸惑いを見せたものの、またいつもの笑顔で応対してくれる。: Karen showed a moment of bewilderment, but then responded with her usual smile.
俺は内心大喜びしながら、かれんの言ってくれた番号を空で覚える。: While feeling overjoyed inside, I memorized the number she gave me.
宏治「なるべく早く掛けますから……」: Koji: “I’ll call as soon as I can...”
かれん「うん、時間のあるときでいいから……」: Karen: “Yeah, when you have time is fine...”
宏治「いや……ちゃんと、すぐに……」: Koji: “No... I’ll do it properly, soon...”

宏治「いやぁ……前任だった小林が退職しまして……全部、私が引き受けることになった: Koji: “Well... Kobayashi, who was my predecessor, resigned... so I ended up taking over everything...”

いくらの仕事なのか皮算用しながら、深々と頭を下げる。: While doing the math in my head to see how much work this would be, I bowed deeply.
高橋「なっ？ いいこともあるだろう？」: Takahashi: “See? Good things happen, don’t they?”
得意げな先生の顔を見て、作り笑いを浮かべるしかなかった。: Looking at the doctor’s smug face, I could only manage a fake smile.

頭を抱えながら記憶の糸をたどり、何回か電話してみたが、それは全て間違った番号だっ: While clutching my head and tracing the threads of my memory, I tried calling several times, but they were all wrong numbers.
その気まずさが、さらに俺たちを疎遠にしていった。: That awkwardness made us drift even further apart.
高橋先生の仕事の受注が決定打となって、俺は晴れて係長への昇進が決まった。: With the decision to accept Dr. Takahashi’s order being the decisive factor, my promotion to Assistant Manager was officially decided.
しかも、大都市の支社への転勤だ……栄転とさえ言える。: What’s more, it’s a transfer to a branch office in a major city... it could even be called a promotion to a prestigious post.
だが引き替えに、俺とかれんは物理的にも遠く離れることになってしまった。: But in exchange, Karen and I became physically far apart.
あれから２年の月日が過ぎ去ろうとしていた。: Since then, two years had passed.
俺はつつがなく支社勤めをまっとうし、次の昇進のシーズンには課長代理への昇格も噂さ: I successfully completed my duties at the branch office, and there are rumors of my promotion to Deputy Manager in the next promotion season. Everything is going smoothly.

かれんが結婚するという噂を風の便りに聞いた……相手は名も知らぬ男だった。: I heard a rumor that Karen was getting married... the partner was a man whose name I didn't know.

アナウンス「お客さまがお掛けになった電話は、現在使われておりません」: Announcement: “The number you have dialed is currently not in use.”

もう一度、似た番号に掛けようとして、思いとどまる。: I thought about calling a similar number once more, but then reconsidered.
（やめておこう……今さらどうなるものでもない……）: (I’ll stop... it’s not like anything will change now...)
再会のチャンスを生かせなかった時点で、俺がかれんと結ばれることはなくなったのだ。: At the point when I couldn't seize the chance for a reunion, any possibility of me being with Karen was gone.
今はもう、諦めるしかない。: Now, I have no choice but to give up.
俺の手がはたと止まる……。: My hands came to a sudden halt...

それに、かれんの顔を見ているとさすがに無理強いはできない。: Besides, looking at Karen’s face, I really can't force myself on her.
壁に押さえつけていたかれんを引き寄せ、ぎゅっと抱きしめる。: I pulled Karen toward me, having pinned her against the wall, and hugged her tightly.
宏治「俺……どうかしてた……許してくれるかな……」: Koji: “I... was out of my mind... I wonder if you’ll forgive me...”
俺はかれんの耳元に口をあて、真面目な声で謝る。: I put my mouth to Karen’s ear and apologized in a serious voice.

かれん「ううん……とりあえず……ここ、出ない？」: Karen: “No... for now... shall we get out of here?”

無言のままのかれん……気まずい空気があたりに漂う。: Karen remained silent... an awkward air drifted around us.

（やっぱり、嫌われたか？）: (As I thought, does she hate me?)

宏治「じゃあ本当に、今日はごちそうさま。お弁当おいしかったよ」: Koji: “Well, really, thank you for the meal today. The lunch was delicious.”
